meta.gate: 4/4 lint pass + 4/4 qa pass + cost>0 + per-tool breakdown preserved + spintax_body preserved for qualitative review
meta.note: D'-a revised gate: agent_tool_calls=0 acceptable if output quality is provably high. Signature-heuristic fix (0a7cab4) active. Copy2 qa.fail: streamline banned word in V1 constraint — copy design limitation. Copy4 v1 qa.fail (0a7cab4): single-newline format caused block-count mismatch. results.label: copy1_growth_signal
results.input_text:
Hi {{firstName}},

I noticed your team posted three new SDR roles this fortnight, and your careers page hints at plans to deploy outbound across two fresh verticals before the next funding cycle wraps.

Best,
Danica

results.label: copy2_walkthrough_streamline
results.input_text:
Hi {{firstName}},

I built a two-minute walkthrough on how four BPOs cut their CV review time from sixteen hours to under three. Worth me sending it your way to streamline your screening?

Best,
Danica

errors: block 2: Variation 1 does not match original paragraph (got 'I built a two-minute walkthrough on how four BPOs cut their '..., expected 'I built a two-minute walkthrough on how four BPOs cut their '...)

results.label: copy3_continuum_metrics
copy4_legal_services_review
results.input_text:
{{firstName}} - noticed {{bad_review_name}} left a 1-star on your google page.
Does it bother you that one more like this could drop your public rating from {{rating}} to {{bad_rating}}?
We help {{practice_area}} firms with strong ratings stay on top by texting clients for reviews after their case is closed. 
Happy clients go straight to Google. Bad feedback comes to you privately instead.
If I offered you five 5-stars (on the house)...would you try it?
{{accountSignature}}
p.s. we helped Fox & Farmer block 3 critiques from going public last month

errors: block count mismatch: got 6 spintax blocks, expected 1 (one per spintaxable input paragraph)
